name: Launch House
about: Launch House is an indoor golf simulator venue that operates on a private rental model, allowing customers to book the entire facility like an Airbnb. The Hillsboro, Oregon location features a single simulator bay with recent updates including a rebuilt enclosure, new auto-tee system, and software upgrades. The space offers a private, self-service experience with remote keypad access and no staff on-site, making it suitable for casual rounds of golf, driving range practice, and swing training sessions. The facility is designed for groups seeking a private golf experience, with options to bring outside food and drinks to enhance the comfort and convenience of their visit.
why_golfers_like_it: Complete privacy with the entire facility reserved for your group
24/7 access for members with remote keypad entry
Updated technology including auto-tee system and simulator software
Ability to bring outside food and drinks to make yourself at home
No-staff required operation for convenient, flexible booking

food_and_drink.notes: Outside food is allowed; BYOK (Bring Your Own Kitchen)
Outside drinks are allowed; BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverages)

simulator_hardware.notes: Featured in simulator packages; one of the available options for hardware
Featured in simulator packages; premium upgrade option for hardware
Featured in simulator packages; more budget-friendly hardware option

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you will need to bring your own golf clubs as they are not provided by the venue.

faq.question: Can I bring food and drink?
faq.answer: Yes, Launch House allows you to bring your own food and drinks to the facility, making yourself at home during your session.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The duration of an 18-hole round can vary, but the new auto-tee system is designed to speed up your pace of play considerably compared to traditional simulators.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is family-friendly and allows juniors, though specific programs or equipment for youth may require checking directly with the location.
